functions.title: Hl main signal or combined main/distant signal
functions.recognition: A full Hl head capable of displaying a red stop aspect and speed information for movement from the signal.
functions.meaning: The display can authorise or prohibit movement at this signal and may simultaneously announce the condition at the next main signal.
playerAction: Treat the lower part as the speed that applies from this signal.
Treat the upper part as advance information for the next main signal.
Stop when the red Hp 0 aspect is displayed.

functions.title: Hl distant-only signal
functions.recognition: An Hl installation providing only advance information. It does not display a red stop aspect and does not impose a new movement authority at its own location.
functions.meaning: It announces the expected aspect or speed at the next main signal.
playerAction: Respond to the upper warning information and brake as required.
Do not interpret the signal as a stop point merely because it shows yellow.

functions.title: Hl distant-signal repeater
functions.recognition: A repeated advance indication positioned to improve sighting. Exact identification equipment and head form must be verified per route.
functions.meaning: Repeats information already given about the next main signal; it does not create a fresh full braking distance.
playerAction: Continue any braking already begun for the announced restriction.
Do not reset your braking plan at the repeater.
notes: Exact TSW artwork and identification markers remain an observation task.

sources.title: DB InfraGO Ril 301.0103 (Hl signal rules)
sources.availability: Referenced through public technical summaries; exact current edition text should be checked where accessible.
